title: GuidelineChunkMetadata
description: Canonical metadata schema for chunked German oncology guideline content with structural, citation, enrichment, and indexing lifecycle metadata.

parent_chunk_id.description: Immediate parent chunk identifier used for parent-section expansion.

root_chunk_id.description: Top-level ancestor chunk identifier for easier hierarchy traversal.

ancestor_chunk_ids.description: Ordered ancestor identifiers from nearest parent to root.

guideline_id.description: Stable identifier for the oncology guideline.

guideline_version.description: Version string of the guideline document.

is_current.description: Whether this chunk belongs to the currently active version of the guideline.

superseded_by_guideline_version.description: Optional newer guideline version that supersedes this chunk's guideline version at the document level.

section_path.description: Ordered section path supporting numeric headings and semantic labels.

is_leaf_chunk.description: Whether this chunk is intended as a retrieval leaf rather than a parent context chunk.

chunk_index_in_parent.description: Order of the chunk within its parent section.

char_start.description: Start character offset of the chunk within the normalized source text.

char_end.description: End character offset of the chunk within the normalized source text.

recommendation_grade.description: Recommendation grade such as A, B, or 0.

evidence_level.description: Evidence level associated with the recommendation, if available.

clinical_intent.description: Clinical intent or care phase, e.g. diagnosis, therapy, follow-up, supportive care.

therapy_setting.description: Treatment setting explicitly referenced, e.g. neoadjuvant, adjuvant, metastatic, palliative.

tumor_stage.description: Tumor staging labels explicitly mentioned in the chunk.

biomarkers.description: Biomarkers, receptor status, or molecular markers explicitly referenced.

diseases.description: Normalized disease or condition mentions referenced in the chunk.

drugs.description: Normalized drug or substance mentions referenced in the chunk.

procedures.description: Normalized procedures, diagnostics, or interventions referenced in the chunk.

patient_subgroups.description: Explicit patient subgroup or demographic mentions present in the chunk.

risk_category.description: Risk categories or stratifications explicitly referenced in the chunk.

reference_ids.description: Reference identifiers cited in the chunk body, extracted from in-text citations such as [1189].

references_cited.description: Structured citation mentions detected in the chunk body.

reference_id.description: Identifier of the cited bibliography entry.

reference_span_start.description: Character offset where the in-text citation begins within the normalized chunk text.

reference_span_end.description: Character offset where the in-text citation ends within the normalized chunk text.

source_filename.description: Original PDF filename or source document identifier.

page_start.description: Starting page number for the chunk.

page_end.description: Ending page number for the chunk.

citation_label.description: Stable citation label used by the answer generation layer and UI source cards.

has_contextual_header.description: Whether an LLM-generated contextual header was prepended before embedding.

has_hypothetical_questions.description: Whether hypothetical retrieval questions were generated for this chunk.

enrichment_version.description: Version tag for the enrichment pipeline that produced retrieval metadata and embedding context.

parser_version.description: Version of the PDF parsing pipeline.

chunking_version.description: Version of the chunking strategy used to produce this chunk.

metadata_extraction_version.description: Version of the entity and metadata extraction pipeline.

indexed_at.description: Timestamp when this chunk was indexed.

source_hash.description: Checksum or content hash of the normalized source span used for debugging and re-indexing.

metadata_confidence.description: Aggregate confidence score for LLM-extracted metadata where such scoring is available.
